230787.explanation: Small-scale recreational stays in rural areas (farm camping, B&Bs, small campsites) offer opportunities for additional/alternative income for farmers. At the same time, we contribute to reducing nitrogen emissions and seek sustainable repurposing and new earning models for agricultural land.

230788.explanation: There have been significant cuts to Refugee Work over the past year. SamenWerkt continues to support existing initiatives aimed at the integration of newcomers and will increase this support where possible.

230790.explanation: Status holders need housing to relieve the overcrowded reception centers. We remain committed to maintaining a good balance with other housing seekers.

230791.explanation: We will actively support the possibility of an energy community (e-hubs) where governments and companies jointly produce, manage, and share sustainable energy. Neighborhood cooperatives will be encouraged.

230792.explanation: We want more greenery in the neighborhoods: green neighborhoods are healthy neighborhoods. We save trees wherever possible; if trees must be cut down, we will replant.

230793.explanation: We believe there are already enough rules.

230796.explanation: We protect our historical and cultural heritage and support accessible ways to share it with people. Where necessary, we apply for European subsidies.

230797.explanation: Of course: enforcement officers (BOAs) must be visible and work focused on neighborhoods and villages. Everyone must be alert to undermining and report suspicious situations. Only together can we tackle this.

230798.explanation: Disagree, due to the high costs associated with this kind of enforcement.

230799.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage, especially for young people and starters. We want to take all measures to ensure that our residents get priority in housing allocation in a fair manner. In any case, more social rental homes must be built.

230800.explanation: Wijk bij Duurstede must remain affordable for young people and starters. Therefore, we stick to at least 35% social rental housing, as laid down in the housing vision (January 2025), and come up with ambitious plans to build homes for young people. By also investing in suitable senior housing (e.g., courtyard homes), we promote housing turnover.

230801.explanation: Together we want to make the municipality more beautiful. That means investing in green spaces, meeting places in neighborhoods, and of course better walking and cycling routes. Zoning rules play an important role in ensuring new buildings fit the environment.

230802.explanation: In serious cases, the obligation to apply for a job can be waived.

230803.explanation: SamenWerkt has positioned itself recently as an advocate for more charging points.

230804.explanation: Residents must be able to park (reasonably), but we must keep an eye on the limits of the city center (and surrounding neighborhoods). We promote an accessible center through (car-restricted) cycling routes, bicycle parking, and a good bus connection.

230807.explanation: The municipality should commit itself to residents with low incomes.

230808.explanation: SamenWerkt wants to explore more forms of citizen participation. A youth council could be a good example.

230809.explanation: SamenWerkt wants to explore more forms of citizen participation. An advisory council could be a good example.

230810.explanation: Actually, this tax is outdated, but given our municipality's financial situation, it is unfortunately still necessary.

230811.explanation: Yes, it is good to involve as many local entrepreneurs as possible. However, (European) procurement rules sometimes pose an obstacle.

230813.explanation: This currently has no priority and should be regulated nationally.

230814.explanation: This is typically something for entrepreneurs to take initiative on themselves, with the VVV (tourist office) providing support.

230815.explanation: The municipality has a responsibility to ensure safe and accessible swimming locations. The Gravenbol and the ferry do not currently meet these requirements. Plans for the Bosscherwaarden already include a city beach. A realistic, scaled-down version makes this feasible.

230817.explanation: We advocate for a fair distribution of responsibilities, taking people's capacity into account. Those who pollute more will pay more. Additionally, processing residual waste is expensive; these costs should be passed on accordingly.

230818.explanation: The question arises: which agreements are we talking about? We support reducing toxins for farmers and all other residents in agricultural areas. However, the municipality has limited influence here.

230819.explanation: A swimming pool would be wonderful, but especially the operating costs are too high for our municipality. We do not want to make false promises to residents. Therefore, we do not choose this option.

230820.explanation: The municipality definitely has a role in early childhood education; however, the costs are high, so SamenWerkt chooses a different approach.

230822.explanation: A theater/film house is indispensable for Wijk bij Duurstede; we will continue to provide structural financial support to Calypso.

230823.explanation: We encourage outdoor play with challenging play environments and nature playgrounds that are accessible to children with disabilities.

230824.explanation: Nobody is against a police post; however, the municipality's influence and mandate lie in 'prevention.' Through neighborhood watch and apps, residents keep their neighborhood or village informed. Enforcement officers (BOAs) must be visible and work focused on neighborhoods/villages. Everyone must be alert to undermining.

230825.explanation: Nobody is against more neighborhood officers; however, the municipality's influence and mandate lie in 'prevention.' Through neighborhood watch and apps, residents keep their neighborhood or village informed. Enforcement officers (BOAs) must be visible and work focused on neighborhoods/villages. Everyone must be alert to undermining.

230827.explanation: Less lighting in rural areas is good for the environment, nature, and energy bills.

230830.explanation: We support splitting houses to increase housing supply. Regulations for care homes should also be relaxed.

230831.explanation: We create housing options for our aging residents, including living with care and small-scale courtyard homes to promote housing turnover. We mainly build small homes for singles, starters/young people, and urgent seekers, focus on 35% social rental, and support housing splitting.

230832.explanation: We mainly build small homes for singles, starters/young people, and urgent seekers, and focus on 35% social rental.

230833.explanation: The benefits scandal shows that algorithms can wrongly accuse innocent people, with major consequences. Such systems are often opaque and error-prone. In such decisions, the human dimension must be paramount.

230834.explanation: This is something the municipality does not need to intervene in. Of course, we do support exchange between businesses and schools.

230835.explanation: Good accessibility is part of good living. We want safe cycling routes and quiet, car-restricted streets. We take smart measures so that cyclists, pedestrians, and cars can safely share the road.

230838.explanation: The cost of investing in a bridge is currently simply too high for a municipality like Wijk bij Duurstede, given the financial challenges in the coming years.

230839.explanation: We are committed to contributing to the national goal of a 50% reduction in greenhouse gases by 2030 (e.g., through wind and solar) compared to 1990, in order to ultimately achieve climate neutrality by 2050. Measures such as providing car-sharing or bike-sharing can help in this.

230840.explanation: We support activities that get elderly people moving and help them out of their isolation.

230841.explanation: We actively combat poverty and debt: early detection, a campaign to break the stigma, and increased awareness of available assistance.

230842.explanation: A dignified farewell deserves a beautiful place.

230843.explanation: We recognize the challenges in rising costs in the social domain, but youth care remains an investment: we provide tailored support while simultaneously seeking smart ways to control costs without losing sight of the individual.

230844.explanation: The problem in Wijk is currently not big enough to prioritize.

230846.explanation: We want to better promote and utilize our rich heritage, with support from European subsidies, and position Wijk bij Duurstede as an archaeological and historical center of Northwest Europe. This offers opportunities for education, tourism, events, and strengthening local identity.

230787.explanation: Every entrepreneur should have the freedom to operate, provided it fits within the environment.

230788.explanation: The VVD can agree with this statement if it concerns at least a (temporary) investment that demonstrably leads to faster integration and the investment equals the maximum effort of the newcomers.

230790.explanation: We must do everything possible to accelerate construction. Also in our municipality.

230791.explanation: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy should definitely be encouraged to do so!

230792.explanation: The VVD believes this depends on the reason, the alternative, and whether the tree is on public or private property.

230793.explanation: In our opinion, it concerns the safety standards.

230796.explanation: The VVD believes that better management of monument care is more important than budget cuts, because we value the historical qualities of our centers and rural areas as important and economically valuable.

230797.explanation: Strict supervision, effective enforcement, and active participation are important to the VVD.

230798.explanation: Strict supervision, effective enforcement, and active participation are important to the VVD.

230799.explanation: As long as there are insufficient homes, the VVD can imagine that, under a balanced consideration, housing seekers from the own municipality are given priority. We must do everything possible to accelerate construction. Also in our municipality.

230800.explanation: The VVD believes building should meet needs, so sufficient flow is created.

230801.explanation: The VVD thinks this does not need to be limited to residential neighborhoods.

230802.explanation: The VVD believes that informal caregivers must remain fully part of (working) society. Not having to apply for jobs also further separates the caregiver from a network and safety net.

230803.explanation: The VVD can agree with this, provided it aligns with a demonstrable need.

230804.explanation: In addition to better enforcement against illegal parking, the VVD believes there should be more parking spaces for entrepreneurs and residents in the city center.

230807.explanation: The VVD believes that debt forgiveness without any return is not a good solution.

230808.explanation: The VVD believes coaching of this youth council is of great importance.

230809.explanation: The VVD also supports a neighborhood or village platform for Wijk and Cothen, similar to the well-functioning one in Langbroek for many years.

230810.explanation: The VVD believes municipal deficits should come from the municipal budget and not be passed on to hardworking residents.

230811.explanation: The VVD believes this is a good thing as long as quality, speed, and costs remain competitive.

230813.explanation: Regardless of whether the VVD would find this a tasteful appeal, the VVD assumes the public can assess such an appeal’s value (or lack thereof).

230814.explanation: The VVD believes entrepreneurs should be perfectly capable of organizing this themselves.

230815.explanation: The VVD believes this should always start with private initiative, supported by the government if necessary.

230817.explanation: The VVD agrees with the statement if the opposite also applies: that people producing less residual waste also pay less. The VVD supports a 'Pay As You Throw' (PAYT) system, as used in many places like Italy.

230818.explanation: The VVD believes that when it comes to toxins, this should be included in all agreements.

230819.explanation: The VVD believes a swimming pool can be built once it is financially sustainable in the long term.

230820.explanation: The VVD believes preschool education is primarily a joint responsibility of parents and municipality, not solely the government.

230822.explanation: The VVD supports Theater Calypso as long as it continues making efforts to attract visitors with appealing programming.

230823.explanation: The VVD believes this good cause can initially be organized well by those involved and residents themselves. Subsequently, the municipality can support where needed.

230824.explanation: The VVD wants more community police officers in Greater Wijk, so police are visible, can work preventively, respond quickly to burglaries, nuisance, and fraud, and together with residents make neighborhoods safer.

230825.explanation: The VVD wants more community police officers in Greater Wijk, so police are visible, can work preventively, respond quickly to burglaries, nuisance, and fraud, and together with residents make neighborhoods safer.

230827.explanation: The VVD believes it must remain livable and safe, also in the rural areas.

230830.explanation: The VVD believes permit-free building on private property, under certain general conditions and for an indefinite period, must definitely be possible.

230831.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality should allow building according to need.

230832.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality should allow building according to need.

230833.explanation: The VVD believes law enforcement agencies must be legally allowed to use technological investigative tools such as algorithms, including in welfare fraud cases.

230834.explanation: The VVD believes that entrepreneurs and educational institutions should first be able to handle this themselves. The municipality can then support this if necessary.

230835.explanation: The VVD believes that accessibility also means that safe accessibility by bicycle should receive attention. A bike street can improve safety and accessibility at the same time.

230838.explanation: The VVD believes that a bike and public transport bridge must be feasible.

230839.explanation: The VVD believes that the municipality should provide space for entrepreneurs to place shared cars or shared bicycles.

230840.explanation: The VVD is of the opinion that this good cause can be well organized by those involved themselves.

230841.explanation: The VVD wonders whether such an approach provides a solution, whether it might actually be biased, and whether the government should interfere at all.

230842.explanation: The VVD does not believe this MUST be done. If private individuals want to do this themselves, the municipality can offer help where desirable and possible.

230843.explanation: The VVD believes we can achieve better results with less money in the interest of the youth.

230844.explanation: The VVD believes adults should be free to choose sex work for themselves, as long as it is legal and voluntary.

230846.explanation: The VVD believes this should be a collaboration between private initiative, the tourist office (VVV), the city of Utrecht, the province, and the municipality.

230787.explanation: The municipality should not always automatically allow this, but assess each situation. Where possible, there should be room for customized solutions and extra activities at farms.

230788.explanation: Integration is not about more money but about a good approach. With clear agreements and proper guidance, newcomers can participate without extra expenses.

230790.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit should not be given priority for social rental housing. Social rental homes are for people who have long been on a waiting list. Priority can cause conflicts and tensions in neighborhoods. That is not good for neighborhood cohesion.

230791.explanation: Generating energy together is good, but support must fit within the municipality's financial capabilities, and those do not exist.

230792.explanation: Trees are important; however, customized solutions are necessary.

230793.explanation: Events are part of a lively municipality, but nuisance must be limited. A good balance is needed.

230796.explanation: Monuments are part of our identity. Budget cuts here undermine the character of the municipality.

230797.explanation: More enforcement officers do not automatically lead to more safety or more trust in the neighborhood. Visibility alone is not enough to solve problems. At the same time, hiring extra enforcement officers costs a lot of money, which is not available. Therefore, we are not clearly for or against more enforcement officers.

230798.explanation: Camera surveillance does not automatically solve safety problems and means collecting data on citizens. That does not fit a safe and free neighborhood. Moreover, it is expensive. Therefore, we are neither for nor against camera surveillance but cautious.

230799.explanation: If you value social cohesion, residents from the municipality itself should have more chances for social rental housing. This helps keep neighborhoods stable and strengthens community quality. People know the neighborhood and feel responsible for it.

230800.explanation: Affordable housing is important and is already included in the housing vision.

230801.explanation: Aesthetic regulations ensure coherence and quality in residential neighborhoods. Without these rules, there is a jumble of buildings that reduces the attractiveness of town centers.

230802.explanation: Informal care is important work. These people must be given room to provide care.

230803.explanation: Charging stations are needed but must not come at the expense of parking spaces for residents.

230804.explanation: The city center must remain well accessible. Research shows parking pressure is already at 100% and that splitting homes is no longer possible. Therefore, more parking space is needed, provided it fits within the available space and livability of the city center.

230807.explanation: Stopping debt forgiveness hits low-income residents hard. This worsens their financial problems and traps them in poverty. The municipality should help people move forward, not push them further into problems.

230808.explanation: Young people must be able to have a say in policy. Not talking about young people, but talking with young people.

230809.explanation: Policy must start with residents and entrepreneurs. By involving them from the beginning, better and more realistic policy emerges. The municipality must listen and collaborate instead of imposing decisions from above.

230810.explanation: The dog tax is unfair and yields little. Residents receive no facilities for it; it is mainly intended to fill budget gaps. Abolishing it reduces burdens and is fairer policy.

230811.explanation: Municipal projects should be carried out as much as possible by local entrepreneurs. This keeps money in the region, strengthens the local economy, and creates jobs. First local, then look further.

230813.explanation: The municipality must be cautious with prohibitions. Freedom and norms must remain balanced.

230814.explanation: A regional label helps local entrepreneurs and makes regional products more visible.

230815.explanation: Recreation is important, but nature, safety, and support must first be carefully considered. The plans are currently strongly linked to sand extraction and sludge dumping, which is an undesirable combination. Without this link, the recreational beach is not feasible.

230817.explanation: Our municipality currently uses diftar, but as a party we support post-sorting. In that system, it is unfair to charge residents extra based on residual waste. Waste separation should be made easier, not more expensive.

230818.explanation: Farmers are partners of the municipality. A general ban on agricultural pesticides is not realistic and can lead to lower yields and risks for the food supply. The agricultural sector already works with strict rules, stays within European standards, and focuses on maximum reduction.

230819.explanation: There is a need for a swimming pool. Due to the closure of pools, residents have to travel far for swimming lessons or sports, creating barriers. A pool promotes health, prevents drowning, and increases available facilities. The municipality should investigate feasibility.

230820.explanation: Early support helps children participate better at school later. The national government is primarily responsible for this, but the municipality has an important role in implementation and reaching families needing extra support.

230822.explanation: The Calypso Theater is an important cultural meeting place and must continue to exist. The municipality must closely monitor whether the theater uses its funds wisely and reaches enough people. This keeps the theater affordable for everyone.

230823.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in sports. Sport helps with health and social participation.

230824.explanation: A police station provides faster assistance and more police visibility. This increases the feeling of safety. Due to centralization, the police are now much less visible in neighborhoods. Therefore, the municipality must actively lobby for a police station.

230825.explanation: Neighborhood police officers are indispensable for trust and safety in the neighborhood. They know the neighborhoods and detect problems early. With enough neighborhood officers, interventions can happen faster and escalation can be prevented. We stand for community-oriented work, close to society.

230827.explanation: Good lighting in rural areas is necessary for safety and accessibility. Less lighting can lead to unsafe situations. The safety of residents weighs more than completely reducing lighting.

230830.explanation: Caregiver homes should be possible so that care can take place nearby.

230831.explanation: Mixed housing for young and old strengthens social cohesion and care in the neighborhood.

230832.explanation: There is a great need for small and affordable housing. These are important for starters and young people, but also for seniors and people with a middle income. The municipality must ensure a varied housing supply that fits different life stages.

230833.explanation: Algorithms can make mistakes and harm residents. Human judgment remains important.

230834.explanation: Cooperation between entrepreneurs and education helps young people find work and companies find staff.

230835.explanation: Bicycle streets can contribute to safe traffic and increased bicycle use. At the same time, we are satisfied with the current balance between bicycles, cars, and pedestrians. New bicycle streets should only be created where really necessary, appropriate, and financially feasible.

230838.explanation: Research into a bridge over the Nederrijn is needed to improve the municipality's accessibility. Provincial roads are congested and traffic jams are increasing. New connections can reduce traffic pressure and make the region more accessible.

230839.explanation: Shared vehicles can help in some situations, but use is limited nationwide. In a rural and extensive municipality, they do not always meet residents' needs. Initiatives must fit the neighborhood and the real demand from residents.

230840.explanation: Good driving skills and safe traffic behavior are important to keep seniors safe and mobile. The municipality should therefore invest in driving skills training, preferably together with partners like Veilig Verkeer Nederland. This should be done within available resources: focused, efficient, and without expensive new programs.

230841.explanation: Shame often prevents people from asking for help. Education can help address problems earlier. Even more important is that caregivers are visibly present in the neighborhood and easily approachable, so problems are quickly noticed and people receive help in time.

230842.explanation: A memorial forest is a nice idea but only feasible with sufficient support and a suitable location. Earlier research showed that this is currently not well realizable.

230843.explanation: Youth care costs are rising too fast. The municipality should focus on prevention, quality, and better organization, not on spending more money continuously.

230844.explanation: The municipality has not granted a permit at this time, so this point is not relevant.

230846.explanation: Wijk bij Duurstede has a rich history, but a large promotional campaign does not fit the municipality's financial situation.

230787.explanation: If an entrepreneur wants to do business, the default attitude should always be: yes, as a municipality we provide the space to do business. And if it proves difficult, the municipality should think along about how it could still be possible.

230788.explanation: The municipality already invests significantly here, together with volunteer organizations. The CDA is pleased that many volunteers are committed to integrating newcomers into our society. Not more money, but more volunteers would be better.

230790.explanation: However, this is only possible if Wijk bij Duurstede receives space from the province to build significantly more homes each year. The numbers of refugees are not the problem, but the housing shortage is.

230791.explanation: The financial situation of the municipality is very poor. The municipality must ensure that our residents who want to become more sustainable together can make maximum use of subsidies from the province and the national government.

230792.explanation: The CDA even wants 800 more trees planted per year. Planting trees can be done by the municipality, but also by residents and entrepreneurs on public land and their own property.

230793.explanation: Strict standards are already in place. It is important to enforce those standards well and to exclude festival organizers who are not professional.

230796.explanation: The financial situation of the municipality is poor. Monument care cannot escape cuts either. The CDA wants to handle cuts to our rich cultural heritage cautiously.

230797.explanation: The CDA wants to expand the number of public order officers (boa's).

230798.explanation: The CDA wants targeted flexible camera surveillance to combat crime and nuisance.

230799.explanation: The CDA wishes our own young people to find housing in our own municipality. That is good for social cohesion. Unfortunately, priority can only be given to a limited extent now.

230800.explanation: The CDA believes more social rental housing should be built but also wants to see more social home ownership.

230801.explanation: The CDA would first like a conversation with residents in such a neighborhood. There is already a lot that does not require a permit.

230802.explanation: Having a paid job is very important. Mandatory job applications help with that. A general exemption is far too simplistic. This should be considered on a case-by-case basis. Part-time work will often make caregiving possible as well.

230803.explanation: The number of electric vehicles is increasing rapidly. This also demands an increase in the number of charging points. The CDA wants public charging points to be bi-directionally usable so that EVs in our municipality can also contribute to stabilizing the power grid.

230804.explanation: With creativity, additional parking spots can probably be found, but the scale will be limited. There is no money to build, for example, an underground parking garage. Parking near a new city beach could be a cheaper alternative.

230807.explanation: Caring for those having difficulty remains important, even if the municipality is financially struggling.

230808.explanation: Good idea to involve young people more in the municipality.

230809.explanation: The CDA fully agrees. Our residents have a lot of knowledge and experience. Better use of this makes living and doing business in our municipality more attractive and results in effective policy with less bureaucratic effort.

230810.explanation: The CDA abolished the dog tax in the past. Unfortunately, due to the very poor financial situation, the tax was reintroduced. As soon as finances allow, the CDA wants to abolish the dog tax again.

230811.explanation: The CDA wants to optimally use local knowledge and craftsmanship. This also results in fewer work vans on the road and extra local income.

230813.explanation: The CDA finds advertising for infidelity unattractive and undesirable. However, it is doubtful whether banning it is legally possible.

230814.explanation: Something like this already exists: Terecht Anders. The CDA cannot estimate whether an additional label would be effective and leaves this to local entrepreneurs and the tourist office (VVV). The entrepreneurs’ fund could pay for this if the entrepreneurs want it.

230815.explanation: The Gravenbol will remain open until a recreational beach is created in the Wijkse Waarden. The condition is that the sand removal from the Bosscherwaarden finances the beach construction and that the beach’s operation is not more expensive than the Gravenbol's.

230817.explanation: This is already part of waste management policy.

230818.explanation: The use of poison is already prohibited in green maintenance. The CDA opposes additional municipal policies regarding the use of crop protection agents by farmers and fruit growers. That should be handled by the national government.

230819.explanation: The municipality has no funds to financially contribute to the operation of a swimming pool. If an entrepreneur wants to build a swimming pool and can operate it without financial support from the municipality, they are more than welcome.

230820.explanation: The municipality does not have the money for this. Currently, there are income-dependent parental contributions.

230822.explanation: Calypso has an important social role. However, it is necessary to also look at how its earning capacity can be increased.

230823.explanation: There is already a lot happening in our municipality. If more offerings are needed, the CDA sees an important role for Stichting Wijksport. But the municipality's financial resources are very limited.

230824.explanation: It would be great if the police station returned. The question, however, is how realistic that is.

230825.explanation: That already happens. It would be great if more neighborhood officers were appointed. Yet the question remains how realistic this is. The alternative, according to the CDA, is extra enforcement officer (boa) capacity. Even in financially difficult times, the CDA wants to allocate money for this.

230827.explanation: Public lighting contributes to safety in rural areas. When replacing lighting in rural areas, the feasibility of sensor-activated lighting should be considered. Off when possible, on when necessary.

230830.explanation: But that is already possible. Pre-caregiver housing will also become allowed within built-up areas. However, outside built-up areas, the province does not yet permit pre-caregiver housing.

230831.explanation: The idea of mixed residential complexes for young and old appeals to the CDA. However, the CDA does not see why the municipality should invest money in this. Often, the elderly are financially capable and the young are not. These generations should be able to manage together without government funding.

230832.explanation: For the CDA, there are two key target groups: young people and the elderly. Young people to move out of the family home, and elderly moving from family homes to apartments. This also ensures housing flow.

230833.explanation: The use of algorithms to detect welfare fraud is only permitted under very strict conditions. According to the CDA, sufficient safeguards have been built in to prevent human rights violations.

230834.explanation: Too little availability of qualified personnel limits the growth of our local businesses. This is crucial for the economic growth of our municipality.

230835.explanation: More cycling to the city center and to amenities in the village.

230838.explanation: Then the charm of the tranquility and the ideal living environment of Wijk bij Duurstede will be lost.

230839.explanation: The municipality does not have the funds for that. It must come from private initiative.

230840.explanation: That is the responsibility of the seniors themselves.

230841.explanation: The CDA supports the goal. SchuldHulpMaatje and Stichting Binding are already doing a lot in this area. Whether it is worthwhile to launch an awareness campaign for this, the CDA cannot assess. They prefer to leave this to the two organizations mentioned.

230842.explanation: The CDA prefers realization through private initiative.

230843.explanation: The CDA wants the municipality to have more control over expenditures. For example, not solving everything through professional care, but by focusing more on strong communities, social contact, and neighborhood activities. The CDA wants better youth care within the current budget.

230844.explanation: Via the General Municipal Ordinance (APV), rules are set for sex workers. This allows the municipality to intervene if there is nuisance for neighbors, but also if there are indications of abuses (human trafficking, underage, etc.).

230846.explanation: The municipality has run out of money. There is still plenty of money in the entrepreneurs’ fund. The CDA calls on entrepreneurs and the museum to come forward with plans and finance them from the entrepreneurs’ fund.

230787.explanation: That is already possible as a side activity. The province's rules are important here.

230788.explanation: That budget must first come from The Hague.

230790.explanation: This fits the regional agreements made. By collaborating with organizations and companies, the municipality is building more social rental and affordable housing for multiple target groups.

230791.explanation: There is no budget for this. However, the municipality can support people with a modest income.

230792.explanation: A tree-for-tree approach is not the best solution. A large canopy is much more important for health and biodiversity.

230793.explanation: The number of noise complaints is minimal. So why become stricter?

230796.explanation: Monuments are the gems of culture and heritage. They make the municipality attractive for everyone.

230797.explanation: Although the municipality is among the safest in the country, enforcement officers (boa’s) can address minor irritations. With the introduction of administrative fines, the money goes to local authorities instead of The Hague.

230798.explanation: Nuisance shifts, which makes cameras expensive. Invest in other means.

230799.explanation: Where legally possible (Cothen and Langbroek), we must preserve that.

230800.explanation: Thanks to the SP, that is already established. Implement those plans!

230801.explanation: That is already possible. The participation of the local environment must be well organized, however.

230802.explanation: That is how we also utilize these forces in society.

230803.explanation: Parking is a problem in some places. Then all drivers must be taken into account.

230804.explanation: Parking pressure occurs at peak times. If more residents come by bike, it will be less. We will investigate possibilities for short-term parking (buy and go).

230807.explanation: A core human value is that we help these people.

230808.explanation: Talk with the youth instead of about the youth. This way we use the strength of young people in society. A youth council can help with this.

230809.explanation: There are enough advisory councils, foundations, and associations that think along and participate. It would be a waste of money and energy to set up a special advisory council.

230810.explanation: Is an outdated principle. Better to tackle dog poop.

230811.explanation: If it fits within the legislation (procurement), local businesses are given preference. Projects must remain affordable.

230813.explanation: Our energy goes to other social issues in our municipality.

230814.explanation: That label already exists through the collaboration within the VVV. Locally, entrepreneurs contribute via LOKL and OKW. By connecting this with other forces from the local and regional community, we make that label even stronger.

230815.explanation: That beach already exists; the Gravenbol. We will only remove it if another beach closer to the city center is provided in its place. And if that is affordable for the municipality.

230817.explanation: The current waste system is a good foundation. National policy does make residual waste more expensive.

230818.explanation: Farmers are already doing very well locally. We use their strength (knowledge and expertise) to further improve health in our rural areas.

230819.explanation: Unaffordable. Unless we look at how to keep construction costs as low as possible. Unfortunately, the council rejected a study on this.

230822.explanation: Theater Calypso is a cultural monument for the entire community, young and old. It is an important showcase of the Wijkse city center and one of the pillars for an attractive business climate.

230823.explanation: Sports should be accessible to everyone, young and old, and for residents with and without disabilities. A study into the needs is a good starting point for this.

230824.explanation: Although losing a desk (due to national police budget cuts) is unfortunate, it costs too much energy and money. We are already too dependent on The Hague's whims. Let's improve safety together locally even better.

230825.explanation: The municipality is among the safest in our country. More police presence on the streets makes the streets even safer.

230827.explanation: Where possible, that is a good idea.

230830.explanation: Aspects of safety and land use are important and therefore assessment is desired. Additionally, the municipality is partly dependent on other governments for much of the area.

230831.explanation: The market only looks at profit. This way, we look again at needs. Together with residents, entrepreneurs, and organizations, we harness strengths to make this possible for society. Housing thus not only generates money but also local enjoyment.

230832.explanation: Building small homes for local starters is one of the goals. Building for local elderly is just as important.

230834.explanation: By working together locally, we harness each other's strengths. Efforts in the region are also aimed at cooperation in the labor market, with other municipalities, employers, employees, and educational institutions.

230835.explanation: Start by improving the dangerous cycling spots. That already strengthens cycling safety in the municipality.

230838.explanation: Good idea. But unaffordable because other governments do not cooperate anyway.

230839.explanation: There are enough organizations that offer this. The municipality can always look at how to support these organizations.

230840.explanation: The national government must arrange this and provide funding. Locally, a municipality can look with elderly organizations at what is possible through cooperation.

230841.explanation: This already happens. We can use even more strengths of associations to break the stigma.

230842.explanation: The space is available. Allow room for private initiatives.

230843.explanation: Young people are the future. They are worth investing in. We talk with young people (prevention) and not just about them. This way, we use their strengths for society.

230844.explanation: The law is the law. So banning is not possible.

230846.explanation: This already happens. With the museum, many monuments and stories (Romans, Vikings, New Dutch Waterline), we are on the map (by UNESCO). By bundling strengths, we enhance local vitality and economic appeal with these showcases.

230787.explanation: Farming is under pressure. An alternative income model must be possible.

230788.explanation: Not sure if money is the most important thing, but the right help is.

230790.explanation: There are more people waiting in line. Everyone must wait their turn.

230792.explanation: Trees and greenery are vital to life!

230793.explanation: To prevent nuisance and hearing damage.

230797.explanation: Police are withdrawing, special enforcement officers (boa's) can fill this gap!

230798.explanation: If it helps to limit the nuisance.

230799.explanation: We have a long list of housing seekers in the municipality.

230800.explanation: There is a great need for social rental housing.

230802.explanation: There are also working people who are also informal caregivers. So there should be no distinction there.

230803.explanation: Electric transportation apparently has the future, and that must be facilitated.

230804.explanation: The city center is not suitable for more parking spaces.

230808.explanation: Young people must join the political party.

230809.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs can turn to the existing political parties.

230810.explanation: Dog tax is a means to increase general revenues. In fact, it should only be a purpose-specific tax to create facilities for dog owners.

230811.explanation: In terms of sustainability and the local economy, we agree with this. The local economy must be supported.

230813.explanation: Adultery only produces losers and should not be encouraged.

230814.explanation: This is a proposal for promoting the local economy.

230817.explanation: In the current system, the polluter already pays.

230820.explanation: Agreed, every child having a good start is very important.

230822.explanation: Calypso Theater is a business and must raise the admission ticket prices.

230823.explanation: Playing sports is healthy and everyone should be able to participate.

230825.explanation: Community police officers and/or public order officers are important for safety and livability.

230827.explanation: In the context of safety, lighting has higher priority than other considerations.

230830.explanation: All initiatives to reduce the pressure on the housing demand must be encouraged.

230831.explanation: Young and old can strengthen each other.

230832.explanation: Make these then lifelong homes, so that elderly can also live there.

230833.explanation: Everything must be done to combat welfare fraud.

230834.explanation: This is important to counter youth unemployment.

230835.explanation: Bicycling is healthy for young and old and reduces the need for parking spaces.

230838.explanation: This is unaffordable and encourages increased traffic, which we do not want.

230839.explanation: This will not work sufficiently, everyone their own car.

230840.explanation: They can also approach parties who want to/can provide this.

230842.explanation: Commemoration has nothing to do with a forest.

230843.explanation: Prevention in youth care is of great importance, for example help with parenting!

230844.explanation: We absolutely do not want to encourage this.

230846.explanation: Utilize cultural-historical values to promote tourist and recreational appeal.

230787.explanation: Yes. D66 supports a sustainable transition of agriculture. In D66's election program (p. 14) it states: "The agricultural sector can strengthen the local economy and employment through a combination of agriculture and tourism/recreation, and thereby also its own sector."

230788.explanation: In our agricultural municipality, healthcare and other sectors face a severe labor shortage. By promoting integration, newcomers contribute to our tight labor market and can start working faster. This increases the livability of the municipality.

230790.explanation: Yes. If the municipality does not do this, the province intervenes at the expense of the municipality of Wijk bij Duurstede. There are legal obligations. The municipality simply must comply with them.

230791.explanation: More than that: not only that, but also the existing arrangements should be expanded if it is up to D66.

230792.explanation: D66 wants a so-called 'replanting obligation', meaning at least one new tree for every felled tree.

230793.explanation: The current legal standards are fine. D66 supports a lively municipality where there is also something for youth to do.

230796.explanation: D66 is a cultural party and wants a lively municipality that is careful with its cultural heritage.

230797.explanation: Local nuisance: fireworks damage, parking violations, dog fouling. Residents suffer from this. That is why D66 supports more municipal enforcement officers.

230798.explanation: Camera surveillance has adverse effects on the privacy of our residents.

230799.explanation: That seems sympathetic. But if surrounding municipalities, such as Houten, Utrechtse Heuvelrug, and Utrecht, follow this example and disadvantage (Wijkse, Cothense or Langbroekse) housing seekers in favor of their own, then it affects our own housing seekers.

230800.explanation: D66 supported (and supports) a minimum of 35% social rental and social homeownership.

230802.explanation: D66 is for prevention. The involvement of a caregiver prevents higher healthcare costs.

230803.explanation: Yes. D66 wants to promote sustainability.

230804.explanation: There must be things to do in the unique and historic city center. Less parking helps with that. D66 supports underground parking or parking just outside the historic city center of Wijk bij Duurstede with its unique character.

230807.explanation: D66 wants to expand debt forgiveness options for the poorest residents.

230808.explanation: The Youth Council is on page 19 of the D66 election program. D66 supports more direct influence from residents on municipal policy, including expanded influence on youth and young people’s policies.

230810.explanation: The levy costs more than it yields.

230811.explanation: There are European tendering rules. Especially for large projects, these rules prohibit favoring certain (local) entrepreneurs in the tender process.

230813.explanation: Freedom of expression is sacred. What one person finds tasteless is acceptable to another. D66 believes the government should be restrained in banning texts.

230814.explanation: D66 supports short supply chains. The 'Kromme Rijn' label is therefore on page 14 of the D66 election program.

230815.explanation: Yes. There must be things to do in Wijk bij Duurstede. We support an open dialogue about this with all stakeholders (page 14 of the D66 election program). The development of Wijkse Waarden offers a unique opportunity for a city beach.

230818.explanation: The municipality must adhere to legal guidelines, among other things to protect groundwater and soil quality. The municipalities of Bunnik and Houten have already announced excluding the use of chemical pesticides in new lease agreements. D66 wants this in Wijk as well.

230819.explanation: The most water-rich municipality in the Netherlands has no swimming pool. That is not a good idea. Prevention and exercise are very healthy for people. This can be achieved by i) using earnings from the energy transition, ii) cooperation with another municipality, and iii) cooperation with companies.

230820.explanation: As an education party, D66 is a strong supporter of this. Equal opportunities for every child.

230822.explanation: As a cultural party, D66 supports this important theater.

230823.explanation: There is little sports provision specifically suitable for this group of people. That is why D66 is in favor.

230824.explanation: Accessibility of ambulance, police, and fire services must be optimal throughout the municipality. A police station does not guarantee services are reachable outside office hours. D66 wants response times to be met throughout the municipality 24 hours a day.

230825.explanation: D66 supports prevention. Having enough community police officers is part of that.

230827.explanation: Due to concerns about social safety, we are not in favor here.

230830.explanation: Building without a permit is not legally feasible.

230831.explanation: This is a task for project developers and housing corporations.

230832.explanation: In view of the severe housing shortage and high starter house prices, D66 supports this.

230833.explanation: The benefits scandal has shown that the use of algorithms can have serious drawbacks. D66 wants social investigation to be based on a targeted suspicion of welfare fraud, not on 'computer says no'.

230834.explanation: This promotes an accessible labor market, also for our residents who have a greater distance from the regular labor market.

230835.explanation: The 'car as guest' principle is supported by D66.

230838.explanation: No, a bridge goes too far. Accessibility and connectivity of the municipality is an important issue for D66. But a bridge would destroy the rural and unique cultural-historical character of the municipality. We are not in favor.

230841.explanation: Shame is a major factor causing residents not to seek (or to delay seeking) help. From a prevention perspective, D66 wants to address this.

230842.explanation: The municipality is responsible for many policy tasks. A memorial forest is sympathetic but is not a municipal policy task.

230843.explanation: No, more money should go to prevention, so that less money is needed for youth care.

230844.explanation: This is not a good idea. A system of permits prevents illegal prostitution, exploitation, or other nuisance. Moreover, a sex worker known to the government can use government services, such as the Public Health Service (GGD).

230846.explanation: The core of Wijk bij Duurstede is one of the most unique cultural-historical sites in Europe. The story of Wijk bij Duurstede should be told even better.

230787.explanation: Local Liberal believes in doing more and having fewer rules, especially for our agricultural sector, which is under significant pressure from national developments.

230788.explanation: First, the influx of asylum seekers must be greatly reduced. We want to first invest in a safe and livable living environment in our municipality for our own residents. Right now, enough money goes to integration.

230790.explanation: The waiting time for residents from Wijkse, Cothense, and Langbroek is already too long. We stand for priority for our people, so that those born or raised here and want to remain living here can do so.

230791.explanation: The grid is full, and we must first look at solutions to ensure our residents and businesses have the certainty of affordable electricity at home and at work.

230792.explanation: We believe in a sustainable and green tree stock. Replacing a felled tree (for example due to disease) fits well with this.

230793.explanation: The noise standards at festivals are legally determined. However, we find it important that residents do not experience unnecessary or prolonged nuisance.

230796.explanation: Municipalities are required to spend money on monument preservation. We cannot cut back further on this.

230797.explanation: Safety is invaluable and the foundation of a good and pleasant life. Enforcement is important in this regard; otherwise, rules remain just rules.

230798.explanation: Every resident must be able to walk safely on the streets. It is scientifically proven that camera surveillance helps to counteract nuisance as well as crime.

230799.explanation: Our residents, especially young people, must have the opportunity to continue living here.

230800.explanation: A percentage of 35 has already been established in the Housing Vision of Wijk bij Duurstede.

230801.explanation: We believe in fewer rules, more action. This way, we want to offer our residents the possibility to make their own decisions about their homes.

230802.explanation: We believe in customized solutions for people receiving social assistance. One caregiver is not the same as another; some provide a lot of care, others less. By having good conversations with people on social assistance, we increase their chances in life and in the labor market.

230803.explanation: At present, there is a large shortage of parking spaces in the municipality. More parking spaces for electric cars could worsen that problem. Therefore, we believe in making at-home charging easier, for example by allowing cable ducts.

230804.explanation: More parking spaces for the inhabitants of the city center. If more parking spaces are needed for tourists, we will first look for solutions further away.

230807.explanation: It has been proven that not forgiving these debts leads to higher costs in the social domain due to an increase in care and debt problems.

230808.explanation: The opinion of our young people is important in our municipality. A youth council can increase interest in politics among young people. This can encourage young people to become council members.

230809.explanation: The gap between residents and politics is too large, and we must seize every possible opportunity to bridge this gap.

230810.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal advocates for the lowest possible burdens on citizens. Life is expensive enough as it is.

230811.explanation: We believe in the local economy and like to encourage, where possible, that our people do our jobs.

230813.explanation: We believe that residents should be free to decide what they do with their lives.

230814.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal strongly believes in the strengths of the beautiful products from our region and municipality. It is fine if municipalities jointly devise a plan for this.

230815.explanation: We consider the Gravenbol a beautiful recreational area and are against sludge dumping in the Bosscherwaarden as a condition for the construction of an urban beach. Sludge dumping carries major pollution risks.

230817.explanation: The current cost charge system for residual waste (diftar) is not a way to reduce the total waste pile. We do not believe in focusing on residual waste and see much more potential in tackling the total waste volume. Post-sorting is easier for our residents and produces a better-sorted waste stream.

230818.explanation: We must give farmers the opportunity to continue farming. However, we do support strongly promoting sustainable solutions in the agricultural sector.

230819.explanation: When financial resources are available again, a new swimming pool is also a serious possibility.

230820.explanation: This is a task of the national government.

230822.explanation: We consider Calypso a wonderful theater and at the same time see commercial growth opportunities to reduce dependence on the municipality.

230823.explanation: We see that sports can greatly improve the lives of people with disabilities in all respects.

230824.explanation: Care and safety must be organized much more locally. The police must be close to our residents. This increases trust in the police and the willingness to report incidents.

230825.explanation: Neighborhood officers are important in the neighborhoods close to residents. We must also look more specifically at addressing particular problems.

230827.explanation: No lighting is not good for traffic safety and the safety of especially women and young people who have to travel through rural areas in the dark.

230830.explanation: We need more housing, especially caregiver homes. But we do believe in some control/oversight of the process.

230831.explanation: We strongly believe in mixed housing complexes for young and old.

230832.explanation: The housing shortage is high. But we must also stimulate the transition from family homes to smaller, life-course-proof homes for the elderly. Construction must also focus on this.

230833.explanation: The childcare benefits scandal has proven that this is not a good idea. The number of people on social assistance is manageable. Moreover, customized solutions in social assistance are of great importance, and good contact with beneficiaries is the start of an improved situation for them. Keep it personal.

230834.explanation: Reducing the gap between supply and demand is always good to tackle. By training students/young people for work in our companies, they can work there and stay living here.

230835.explanation: When establishing the environmental vision, traffic safety, accessibility, sustainability, and costs are taken into account. Lokaal Liberaal wants to coordinate these matters as well as possible. A bicycle street will certainly be included.

230838.explanation: Research has already been done here in the past and a decision was made not to build a bridge. Expensive new research is pointless. A bridge would bring much more traffic through Wijk bij Duurstede and spoil the city’s appearance, whereas the current ferry connection fits the unique character of our city.

230839.explanation: At this time, there are enough shared cars for the existing demand in Wijk bij Duurstede.

230840.explanation: People are responsible for their own driving skills.

230841.explanation: Anyone can fall into debt. We believe this is nothing to be ashamed of and see the added value of a municipality that addresses this openly and continuously, and provides help.

230842.explanation: This is not a priority for Lokaal Liberaal, but we will also not vote against it if it can be implemented without costs to the municipality—for example, through sponsorship or donations.

230843.explanation: Youth care is important. The youth are the future. The care system is completely broken down because our youth need more and more care. It is a statutory task. We want to use the funds provided by the national government better, with fewer layers in between, thus making it cheaper.

230844.explanation: Banning sex work can encourage an illegal circuit. This results in no oversight and makes the situation unsafe for sex workers. Everyone should be able to choose their own profession and perform it safely.

230846.explanation: Our municipality has a rich and historically significant position in European history. Our municipality benefits more from ongoing promotion than from a one-time campaign.

statements.title: The municipality should create more parking spaces in the city center of Wijk bij Duurstede.
The municipality should prohibit the use of pesticides/toxic chemicals in new agreements with farmers.
A youth council should be established in Wijk bij Duurstede.

titleParts.text: In Wijk bij Duurstede moet een

titleParts.glossary: A group of young people who provide input and discuss plans and policy with the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.theme: Priority for people with refugee status
statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the municipal aesthetic rules for building and renovating in more residential neighborhoods. In these neighborhoods, construction would be allowed without requirements on, for example, color or shape.

titleParts.glossary: Municipal rules that determine how the houses and gardens in a neighbourhood should look.

titleParts.text: voor bouwen en verbouwen in meer woonwijken afschaffen. In deze wijken mag zonder eisen aan bijvoorbeeld kleur en vorm worden gebouwd.

statements.title: Camera surveillance should be installed in the municipality at places with a lot of nuisance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: komen op plekken waar veel overlast is.

statements.title: Wijk bij Duurstede should set up an advisory council with residents and entrepreneurs.
statements.moreInfo: Wijk bij Duurstede has an advisory council for the social domain. It includes residents from Wijk bij Duurstede, Cothen and Langbroek who each bring their own knowledge and experience in care and welfare. The advisory council advises the mayor and aldermen, both when asked and unasked. The advice covers a wide area, namely the social domain — for example loneliness, access to mental health care, poverty and debt assistance, citizen initiatives and informal care. There is currently no broader advisory council that also includes, for example, entrepreneurs.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Wijk bij Duurstede should, together with other municipalities in the Kromme Rijn region, work towards a "Kromme Rijn" label. With that label, local entrepreneurs can sell regional products.
statements.moreInfo: Farmers can cooperate to promote regional products. One way to do this is with a label, a kind of quality mark. That lets buyers know from which region the products come. There is currently no specific label for products from Wijk bij Duurstede.

statements.title: There should be a recreational beach in the Wijkse Waarden.

titleParts.glossary: A beach where you can walk, play football, swim, sunbathe, etc.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.

titleParts.text: van de gemeentelijke belastingen voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should make preschool education free for all children.

titleParts.glossary: A program for children aged 2½ to 4 years that stimulates their development in various areas. It is offered at the playgroup or at childcare.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Residents who produce a lot of residual (non-recyclable) waste should pay more.
The municipality should invest money in expanding sports offerings for people with physical or mental disabilities.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in het uitbreiden van het sportaanbod voor mensen met een lichamelijke of geestelijke

titleParts.glossary: Something that hinders you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual, related to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in mixed housing complexes for young and old.
The municipality should actively lobby for a police station in Wijk bij Duurstede.
statements.moreInfo: At the town hall of Wijk bij Duurstede you can visit the police support point on Mondays and Thursdays. On other days residents can go to the police station in Doorn. The police are of course always reachable by phone. There are also neighborhood officers in the municipality. Until about ten years ago there was a police station in the municipality. The introduction of the National Police meant the end of several police teams across the country and also led to the closure of the police station in Wijk bij Duurstede.

statements.title: Public lighting in the rural area should be turned off more often.

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: The municipality should ban advertising that promotes adultery in public spaces.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet reclame die oproept tot

titleParts.glossary: Having a (sexual) relationship with someone other than one's steady partner, without that partner's consent.

statements.title: The municipality should not use algorithms to detect social assistance (welfare) fraud.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geen gebruik maken van

titleParts.glossary: A step-by-step plan or a list of rules that a computer follows to carry out a task or solve a problem.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the construction of mantelzorg homes (family caregiver homes) on private property without a permit.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het realiseren van

titleParts.glossary: A self-contained dwelling on the grounds of another house, where, for example, grandparents can be cared for.

titleParts.text: en op eigen terrein zonder vergunning toestaan.

statements.title: The municipality should ensure that a shared car or shared bicycle is available in every neighborhood.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet ervoor zorgen dat er in iedere wijk een

titleParts.glossary: Car that multiple people can use.
Bicycle that multiple people can use.

statements.title: A memorial forest should be established in the municipality of Wijk bij Duurstede.

titleParts.glossary: A forest where trees are planted in memory of deceased loved ones.

titleParts.text: komen in de gemeente Wijk bij Duurstede.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate more money to youth care.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uittrekken voor

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in driving-skill training for seniors for cars, bicycles, and mobility scooters.
The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign for Wijk bij Duurstede as an archaeological and historical center of Northwestern Europe.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.

titleParts.text: voor Wijk bij Duurstede als archeologisch en historisch centrum van Noordwest-Europa.

statements.moreInfo: Wijk bij Duurstede is a "small city with a big history." Wijk bij Duurstede experienced three notable historical periods: the Roman period, the Wijk Golden Age and the Burgundian period. There are many sites and buildings connected to that history, such as Duurstede Castle, the Dorestad Museum, the city harbor and the historic buildings. Many people are interested in history, which can be a reason for tourists to visit Wijk bij Duurstede.

statements.title: The municipality should research the feasibility of a bridge over the Nederrijn.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
At least 35% of the newly built homes in Wijk bij Duurstede should be social rental housing. Currently, 22.9% of the homes in the municipality are social rental housing.
Noise limits at festivals should be stricter.

shootoutStatements.theme: Exemption from the job application obligation
shootoutStatements.title: People on social assistance who care for a family member or acquaintance as a caregiver should not be required to apply for jobs.
The municipality should mainly build small houses and apartments for first-time buyers and renters.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The municipality should collaborate with businesses and educational institutions to better align labor market supply and demand.
The municipality should involve local businesses as much as possible in major projects, for example in housing construction.
The municipality should continue to financially support the Calypso Theater.
The municipality should ask the police to provide more neighborhood police officers.
The municipality should stop issuing permits to sex workers.
The municipality should designate more streets as bicycle streets.
Wijk bij Duurstede should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open a shop or campsite at their farm.
There should be a swimming pool in Wijk bij Duurstede.
The municipality should set up an information campaign to break the stigma around poverty and debt.
